title: Sift raises $42 million in Series B round
summary:
WASHINGTON – Sift, a Southern California startup developing tools to help engineers make sense of hardware sensor data, raised $42 million in a Series B investment round. With the funding, Sift plans to expand its staff of engineers building the infrastructure layer that underpins devices controlled by artificial intelligence algorithms.   “Modern rockets, satellites, defense […]

title: Airbase raises $5 million to tackle spectrum bottleneck as FCC eyes new space uses
summary:
New York-based software startup Airbase emerged from stealth March 25 after raising $5 million to modernize how governments coordinate radio frequencies used by satellites, 5G networks and other wireless systems.

title: Pave Space raises $40 million to develop European heavy kickstage
summary:
Swiss startup Pave Space has raised $40 million to develop an orbital transfer vehicle that could move satellites from LEO to their final destinations in hours instead of months.

title: NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations
summary:
NASA, concerned about slow development of commercial markets, is considering revamping its strategy for supporting the creation of commercial space stations to succeed the International Space Station.
The post NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Amazon wildfire emissions up to three times higher than estimated
summary: Wildfires that swept across the Amazon in 2024 were the most devastating in more than two decades. New research funded by the European Space Agency (ESA) suggests emissions may have been up to three times higher than earlier estimates.

title: Kona Storms Flood Oʻahu
summary: Back-to-back subtropical cyclones in March fueled destructive flash flooding on several of the Hawaiian Islands.

title: NASA outlines ambitious $20 billion plan for moon base
summary: Along with plans for a moon base, senior NASA managers also outlined work to develop nuclear power systems for use on the moon and Mars to keep astronauts, habitats and other equipment warm while providing the electricity needed for research, construction and daily operations.
